TTIS Admin & Operations Guide

Audience: TTIS administrators/operators (CEO + ops). Scope: setup, deploy, configuration, management, and troubleshooting for every TTIS system — the VBS backend, the Dashboard, the Field App, GCP/Caddy, the marketing site, the LMS, the RAG corpus, and Ask‑LYNNE. Last updated: 2026‑06‑16.

Conventions used below - Commands are PowerShell-friendly on Windows dev boxes; the GCP host is Linux (bash). - H:\Projects\… are the local working copies; ~/ttis-ai is the repo on the GCP VM. - "VBS" = the FastAPI backend (ttis-ai). "Dashboard" = the Next.js app (TTI-S_Dashboard). - Anything marked (verify) is an expected/observed value — confirm against the live system.

0. Overview & Architecture

0.1 The systems and where they live

System Repo / path Stack Prod surface
TTI‑VBS (backend) H:\Projects\TTI-S_VBS\ttis-ai Python 3.11, FastAPI app.tti-solutions.com/api/*, webhook.tti-solutions.com
TTI‑Dashboard (web UI) H:\Projects\TTI-S_Dashboard Next.js 15, TypeScript app.tti-solutions.com
TTIS Field App H:\Projects\ttis-field-app Expo / React Native (native iOS+Android) App stores (EAS) — talks to app.tti-solutions.com
Marketing site ttis-ai\website Static (Markdown → HTML) tti-solutions.com, www.…
LMS part of VBS (dashboard/lms.py) FastAPI module + Dashboard tabs app.tti-solutions.com (LMS tabs)
RAG corpus ttis-ai\data\documents + ChromaDB ChromaDB + hybrid BM25 internal (port 8002)
Ask‑LYNNE part of VBS (dashboard/lynne_*) Claude Opus + SSE app.tti-solutions.com (Ask‑LYNNE tab), Telegram

0.2 How prod fits together (single GCP VM, Caddy host-routing)

                         ┌──────────── GCP VM: ttis-prod (us-central1-a) ────────────┐
   Internet  ──DNS──►    │  Caddy (TLS / Let's Encrypt, host-based routing)          │
                         │    tti-solutions.com / www      → static file_server      │
   app.tti-solutions.com │    app.tti-solutions.com        → /api/* → dashboard:8000 │
   www.tti-solutions.com │                                   /*     → frontend:3000  │
   webhook.tti-…         │    webhook.tti-solutions.com     → webhook:8001           │
                         │                                                          │
                         │  docker compose:  dashboard(8000) frontend(3000)          │
                         │                   webhook(8001) worker  redis(6379)       │
                         │                   chromadb(8002)  caddy                   │
                         │  Cloud SQL (Postgres) via DATABASE_URL                    │
                         └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
  • COMMAND‑CORE (orchestration/command_core.py) is a deterministic event→workflow router (not an LLM).
  • Agents (~34, config/agents.yaml) are Claude-backed (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus tiers).
  • Execution mode: inline (dev, in-process) vs queue (prod, Redis + python -m orchestration.worker).

0.3 Prerequisites (admin workstation)

  • Git, Python 3.11+, Node 20+, Docker Desktop (Compose v2).
  • Access: GitHub org TTIScharlesT, the GCP project tti-solutions-ai, the Anthropic console.
  • A populated config/.env for the VBS (never committed). See §1.2.

1. TTI‑VBS (Backend)

1.1 Setup

cd H:\Projects\TTI-S_VBS\ttis-ai
python -m venv .venv
.venv\Scripts\activate
python -m pip install --upgrade pip
pip install -r requirements.txt
Copy-Item config\.env.template config\.env   # then edit (see §1.2)

Generate the two mandatory secrets:

python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(48))"   # → DASHBOARD_JWT_SECRET (>=32 chars)
python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(24))"   # → DASHBOARD_PWD_SALT  (>=16 chars)

1.2 Configuration — config/.env

The full template is config/.env.template. The variables that matter most:

Variable Required Purpose
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Yes Claude API key (all agents + LYNNE).
DASHBOARD_JWT_SECRET Yes JWT signing key, ≥32 chars, never a placeholder.
DASHBOARD_PWD_SALT Yes Password hash salt, ≥16 chars.
DASHBOARD_USERS first boot JSON seed of users (after first boot the auth.db is source of truth).
WORKFLOW_EXECUTION_MODE prod inline (dev) or queue (prod).
REDIS_URL queue mode e.g. redis://localhost:6379/0 (redis://redis:6379/0 in Docker).
DATABASE_URL prod unset → SQLite; postgresql://… → Cloud SQL.
CORS_ALLOW_LIST web UI Must include the Dashboard origin (e.g. https://app.tti-solutions.com).
SCHEDULER_ENABLED opt‑in 1 starts the recurring-job loop (reminders, daily updates). Default 0.
SCHEDULER_TZ opt‑in e.g. America/Chicago — daily job times are local.
Deploy/domains prod APP_DOMAIN, DASHBOARD_PUBLIC_URL, WEBSITE_DOMAIN, ACME_EMAIL, WEBHOOK_DOMAIN, FRONTEND_UPSTREAM.
Integrations optional PURSUITE_*, DUFFEL_API_TOKEN, GOOGLE_*, GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY, TICKETMASTER_API_KEY, SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL, OWNER_EMAIL.

Optional/unset integration keys degrade gracefully — the feature just isn't offered.

1.3 Run — dev (inline mode), 3 terminals

# 1) Bootstrap (loads .env, inits all SQLite DBs, builds tool + agent registries, COMMAND-CORE)
python main.py

# 2) Dashboard API (port 8000)
uvicorn dashboard.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000

# 3) Webhook receiver (port 8001) — Pursuite CRM + Telegram inbound
uvicorn tools.pursuite_webhook:webhook_app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8001

python main.py prints a startup banner ending with the service URLs and the two uvicorn commands. Health check: curl http://127.0.0.1:8000/health → 200.

One-shot maintenance commands (don't start a server):

python main.py --ingest-docs      # ingest documents into ChromaDB (see §7)
python main.py --verify-chain     # verify the append-only audit hash chain
python main.py --list-gates       # list workflows awaiting a human gate
python main.py --test-pursuite     # test Pursuite CRM connectivity

1.4 Run — Docker (queue mode, production shape)

docker-compose up -d           # chromadb:8002, redis:6379, dashboard:8000, webhook:8001, worker
docker-compose logs -f         # tail
docker-compose up -d --scale worker=3   # more queue throughput
docker-compose down            # stop (add -v to wipe volumes)

In queue mode the API/webhook enqueue to Redis (queue:workflow_jobs) and the worker consumes them: python -m orchestration.worker. Inline mode runs the workflow in a background thread (dev only — not durable across restarts).

Docker Desktop / WSL2 gotcha — single‑file .env bind mounts. On Windows, bind‑mounting a single file (e.g. ./.env:/app/.env) is unreliable: Docker Desktop can create the destination as a directory, and then runc rejects the mount with not a directory: Are you trying to mount a directory onto a file (or vice‑versa)? — typically on restart of a container that was first created when the mount existed. Fixes: - Prefer env_file: over a single‑file bind mount — it injects the vars with no filesystem mount. - If you hit it, recreate (don't restart) the container so it picks up the current config: docker compose down --remove-orphans && docker compose … up -d --force-recreate. Never docker start/restart the stale container — that replays the dead mount. - If Docker auto‑created a bogus directory at the host .env path, delete it and ensure a real file exists (copy from .env.template) before bringing the stack back up.

1.5 Data layer

  • SQLite (dev/Railway) in data/sqlite/: audit.db (append-only, chain-verified — never disable), auth.db, state.db, finance*.db, hr.db (PII-guarded), contracts.db, schedule.db, plus domain + safety DBs. Tables are created idempotently on boot.
  • PostgreSQL (prod GCP Cloud SQL) when DATABASE_URL=postgresql://…. db.py adapts placeholders and DDL automatically; no app code changes.
  • ChromaDB (RAG vector store) — data/chroma_db/, container port 8002.
  • Redis — task queue + workflow context + telemetry.

1.6 Manage

  • Scheduler (orchestration/scheduler_runner.py): set SCHEDULER_ENABLED=1 to start the in‑process tick loop. Seeds the CEO daily‑update job (if DAILY_UPDATE_RECIPIENTS is set) and the timecard reminder/supervisor‑digest jobs. Knobs: SCHEDULER_TZ, SCHEDULER_TICK_SECONDS, TIMECARD_REMINDER_INTERVAL_SECONDS, TIMECARD_SUPERVISOR_DIGEST_TIME.
  • Agent kill‑switch (orchestration/agent_flags.py): durable disable/enable per agent, broadcast over Redis; consulted before each agent run.
  • Useful scripts (scripts/): migrate_db_files.py, setup_google_workspace.py, setup_staging_env.py, register_telegram_webhook.py, seed_wizards.py, seed_lms_catalog.py, seed_policy_ack.py, ingest_rag_corpus.py, scaffold_corpus.py, build_agent_guide.py, build_workflow_guide.py, deploy_prod.sh.

1.7 Test

.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/ -q          # full suite
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/test_abac_pdp.py -q   # subset

Integration/realtime tests need Redis:

docker-compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up -d         # ephemeral Redis on 6399
$env:REDIS_URL = "redis://localhost:6399/0"
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/test_realtime_integration.py -q
docker-compose -f docker-compose.test.yml down -v

CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) runs the full guardrail + integration suite on every push/PR with an ephemeral Redis, Python 3.11.

1.8 User & password management (local/dev)

Identities live in auth.db. The password hash is sha256(DASHBOARD_PWD_SALT + plaintext) — use the users helpers so the salt is applied for you. To reset the tester's password and create/refresh a charles CEO login:

cd H:\Projects\TTI-S_VBS\ttis-ai
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -c "
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv('config/.env')
from dashboard.users import init_users_db, set_password, upsert_user, hash_password, get_user
from dashboard.abac import Role
init_users_db()
PW = 'TTIS-CEO-2026!'
print('reset tester (U-TEST):', set_password('U-TEST', PW))
upsert_user(user_id='U-001', username='charles', password_sha256=hash_password(PW),
            role=Role.parse('ceo'), domain='tti-solutions.com')
u = get_user('charles')
print('charles present:', bool(u), '| role:', u['role'] if u else None)
"

(On the prod VM the equivalent runs inside the container with docker exec — see §4.6.)

1.9 Local dev against the seeded DB

To exercise the real seeded data end‑to‑end on your workstation:

# Backend on :8000, loading config/.env explicitly
cd H:\Projects\TTI-S_VBS\ttis-ai
.\.venv\Scripts\activate
uvicorn dashboard.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000 --env-file config\.env

# Frontend on :3000 (separate terminal), then HARD-refresh the browser (Ctrl+F5)
cd H:\Projects\TTI-S_Dashboard
git pull
npm run dev          # or: npm run build; npm start

The --env-file config\.env flag is what loads your keys when starting uvicorn directly (without it the backend boots with no secrets and fails closed). After a fresh frontend build, hard‑refresh to drop the cached bundle or you'll see stale UI.

1.10 Troubleshoot

Symptom Diagnose Fix
Startup aborts: JWT secret missing/short/placeholder the error names the var Set a ≥32‑char DASHBOARD_JWT_SECRET (§1.1).
Backend boots with no auth / 401 everything started uvicorn without env Add --env-file config\.env (§1.9).
ModuleNotFoundError wrong/own venv not active .\.venv\Scripts\activate; pip install -r requirements.txt.
Redis connection refused redis-cli ping fails docker-compose up -d redis; check REDIS_URL.
Workflows enqueue but never run redis-cli llen queue:workflow_jobs grows Start python -m orchestration.worker (or --scale worker).
Audit chain "tampered" python main.py --verify-chain reports a break Do not disable — investigate; the chain is inviolable by design.
403 on all routes user/role not provisioned Create a user (§1.8); verify ABAC role in auth.db.
Login fails after salt change old hashes no longer match Re‑set passwords with the helper (§1.8) — it re‑hashes with the current salt.
CORS errors from the web UI browser console shows blocked origin Add the origin to CORS_ALLOW_LIST, restart VBS.
Port already in use (:8000/:3000/:8081) another process holds it Kill it — Windows one‑liner in the Appendix.
LYNNE/agents 429 Anthropic rate limit Auto‑retries with back‑off; raise the org token/min if persistent.

1.11 Security & regression testing (MTH + pipeline)

Mutative Test Harness (MTH, R‑MTH‑01) — adversarial, in‑process RBAC/auth/injection fuzzing (tools/mth/). It mints a JWT per role, fires mutated requests at the app via TestClient, and an oracle grounded in dashboard/abac.py::can() flags any divergence — so it's self‑checking against the live policy.

python -m tools.mth                  # full run → data/mth/latest.{json,html}
python -m tools.mth --dry-run        # build the plan, send nothing
python -m tools.mth --out DIR        # write the report elsewhere

Strategy groups: Protocol (missing‑auth / forged alg=none / malformed), RBAC (wrong‑role), Structural (overpost / remove‑field / empty‑object), Type (coercion / binary), Value (SQLi / XSS / path‑traversal / long‑string), Semantic (cross‑entity TTIS↔ROSS_LV), Chaos (1 MB / duplicate), Injection (login SQLi). CRITICAL findings (AUTH_BYPASS, RBAC_BYPASS, INJECTION_ACCEPTED, OVERPOST_ESCALATION, CROSS_ENTITY_LEAK) → exit 1; UNEXPECTED_CRASH/IDEMPOTENCY_VIOLATION (HIGH) and MISSING_VALIDATION (LOW) are reported, not blocking. It seeds a real charge code + a timecard advanced through the approval chain so write/approval routes hit valid targets. Add an endpoint = add a Baseline in tools/mth/baselines.py (the engine auto‑generates every variant). CI gates on it (tests/test_mth_oracle.py + a step that fails on CRITICAL and uploads the report artifact).

Regression pipeline (R‑REG‑01) — one command runs the whole suite as ordered phases + the MTH gate + a summary report:

python scripts/run_regression.py                 # or:  .\scripts\run-regression.ps1
python scripts/run_regression.py --list          # phases + file assignment
python scripts/run_regression.py --phase auth_identity
python scripts/run_regression.py --skip-realtime --skip-mth --filter timecard

14 phases (guardrails, auth_identity, timecards, finance_payroll, field_ops, projects_pm, lms_onboarding, lynne_eaa, agents_workflows, wizards, travel, integrations, security, realtime) + the MTH harness; the realtime phase auto‑skips when Redis is unreachable. Report → test-results/ regression-<ts>.txt; exit 0/1. (Plain pytest tests/ -q still works for ad‑hoc runs.)

2. TTI‑Dashboard (app.tti-solutions.com)

2.1 Setup

cd H:\Projects\TTI-S_Dashboard
npm ci                       # clean install from package-lock (Node 20+)
Copy-Item .env.example .env.local   # then edit (see §2.3)

2.2 Run — dev

npm run dev                  # http://127.0.0.1:3000  (use 127.0.0.1, not localhost — cookie SameSite)

The Dashboard talks to the VBS API at NEXT_PUBLIC_VBS_BASE_URL (dev: http://127.0.0.1:8000). The VBS backend's CORS_ALLOW_LIST must include the Dashboard origin.

2.3 Configuration

Variable Dev value Purpose
NEXT_PUBLIC_VBS_BASE_URL http://127.0.0.1:8000 VBS API base (prod: https://app.tti-solutions.com).
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL http://localhost:3000 The app's own public origin.
NEXT_PUBLIC_STEPUP_THRESHOLD_USD 100000 Step‑up MFA threshold for high‑value actions.
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID / GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET optional Google OAuth (restricted to tti-solutions.com).
ROLE_MAP dev/staging JSON email→role override until backend supplies role claims.
*_DEMO flags dev Seed sample data (agents/workflows/finance/etc.) with no backend.

NEXT_PUBLIC_* values are build‑time for the prod image (baked by CI); runtime secrets (OAuth, OTEL) are injected at container start.

2.4 Build & test

npm run typecheck            # tsc --noEmit
npm run lint                 # eslint --max-warnings 0
npm run test:cov             # vitest + coverage (gate: 80% global; 100% on abac/ + finance/)
npm run build                # .next/standalone
npm run e2e                  # Playwright (desktop / Pixel 7 / tablet)

CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) runs lint → typecheck → test:cov → build → e2e. Do not run npm audit fix --force (it can downgrade Next.js and break the App Router).

Smoke‑test against a running VBS backend (scripts/vbs-smoke*.mjs — pure Node, hits the API over HTTP; the backend must be up and reachable at NEXT_PUBLIC_VBS_BASE_URL):

cd H:\Projects\TTI-S_Dashboard
$env:VBS_SMOKE_USER="charles"
$env:VBS_SMOKE_PASS="<your VBS password>"
npm run vbs:smoke                 # full smoke; or a slice: npm run vbs:smoke:timecard / :payroll / :lynne / …
Remove-Item Env:VBS_SMOKE_PASS    # clear the password from the shell when done

Slices available: vbs:smoke:v3 / :timecard / :daily-log / :incident / :session / :phase3 / :uam / :phase5-obs / :phase4 / :rag / :lynne / :rag-resolver / :agent-mgmt / :history / :payroll.

2.5 Deploy

  • On every push to main, .github/workflows/ghcr.yml builds and pushes a Docker image to GHCR (ghcr.io/ttischarlest/tti-s_dashboard:latest) with the prod NEXT_PUBLIC_* baked in.
  • The VBS deploy script pulls that image onto the GCP VM (docker compose pull frontend) — see §4.
  • Local Windows redeploy helper: ./scripts/run-redeploy.ps1 [-Ref main] [-Port 3000].

2.6 Debugging a failing API call in the browser (DevTools)

When the UI misbehaves, the fastest diagnosis is to watch the actual request the browser made.

Open DevTools (Windows — Chrome/Edge/Firefox): - F12 (or Ctrl+Shift+I) — DevTools. - Ctrl+Shift+J — straight to the Console. - Firefox Ctrl+Shift+E — straight to Network. (Mac: Cmd+Option+I.)

Capture the failing call: 1. F12 → Network tab. 2. Click the Fetch/XHR filter (hides images/CSS, shows just API calls); optionally type api/v1 in the filter box. 3. Leave DevTools open, then click the panel in the app (or Ctrl+R to reload). 4. Look for rows in red or with a 4xx/5xx Status — those are the failures. 5. Click a red row → the Status (top of Headers), the Request URL, and the Response tab tell the story.

Common reads: a 4xx/5xx with no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header → CORS (fix the VBS CORS_ALLOW_LIST); 401 → auth/token; 404 on a new endpoint → backend not redeployed; net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED → wrong NEXT_PUBLIC_VBS_BASE_URL or VBS not running.

2.7 Troubleshoot

Symptom Diagnose Fix
API calls fail / blocked (CORS) DevTools Network: missing Access-Control-Allow-Origin Add the Dashboard origin to VBS CORS_ALLOW_LIST, restart VBS.
Login 401 DevTools Network: /api/v1/auth/login → 401 Verify creds; check VBS health.
New endpoint 404 from the UI DevTools Network: 404 on /api/v1/… Redeploy backend (GCP §4.4); confirm main is current.
ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED wrong base URL / VBS down Fix NEXT_PUBLIC_VBS_BASE_URL; start VBS on :8000.
Coverage gate fails vitest report; abac/+finance/ <100% Add tests; those paths require 100%.
Old UI after deploy browser bundle cache Hard refresh (Ctrl/Cmd‑Shift‑R); confirm frontend image was pulled.
Next downgraded to 9.x npm audit fix --force was run Revert package.json/lock, npm ci.

3. TTIS Field App

A standalone native app (H:\Projects\ttis-field-app, Expo ~54 / React Native, iOS+Android) — not a browser page. Per ADR‑0001 a native app was chosen over PWA for reliable push (P1 safety alerts) and durable on‑device storage of unsynced DCAA timecards. Crews use the installed app; it is a thin client over the same VBS API (https://app.tti-solutions.com, set in app.json → extra.apiBaseUrl). Scope doc: ttis-ai/docs/MOBILE_FIELD_APP_SCOPE.md.

3.1 Setup & run (dev)

cd H:\Projects\ttis-field-app
npm install
npm run start                # Expo dev server + QR (open in Expo Go)
npm run ios                  # iOS simulator
npm run android              # Android emulator
npm run web                  # react-native-web preview

3.2 What a field user gets

  • Login with VBS credentials (field_crew role); JWT stored in expo-secure-store (Keychain/Keystore).
  • Tabs: Home, Time, Schedule, Pay, Assistant (FIELDAIDE — a read‑only field agent).
  • Submit forms: timecard, daily log, incident (with tappable body‑map), travel — all exactly‑once (Idempotency‑Key). All reads are server‑scoped to the caller's user_id (a tech only sees their own).
  • Backend endpoints: /api/v1/timecards/summary, /api/v1/payroll/next-payday, /api/v1/schedule/me, /api/v1/field/assistant, /api/v1/projects/assigned, plus the standard /timecards, /daily-logs, /incidents, /travel/requests submits.

3.3 Build & distribute (EAS)

npm install -g eas-cli
eas login
eas build --platform ios       # → TestFlight
eas build --platform android   # → Play Internal Testing
eas update                     # OTA JS update (expo-updates)

Status: P0 (auth + tab shell + live data) and P1 (submit forms) shipped. Pending: P3 push (device registry + mission‑critical inbox) and P4 hardening (offline queue, biometric unlock, EAS pilot).

3.4 Troubleshoot

Symptom Cause Fix
Can't reach API in dev pointing at prod by default App uses app.tti-solutions.com; for local VBS, override extra.apiBaseUrl / lib/config.ts.
Login fails VBS down or wrong role Verify VBS health; user must have field_crew role.
Expo Go won't connect phone/PC not same LAN Same network, or use a tunnel (expo start --tunnel).

4. GCP, Deploy & Domains

4.1 Production host

  • GCP project tti-solutions-ai, VM ttis-prod (zone us-central1-a, Ubuntu). Repo at ~/ttis-ai.
  • Live IP: 34.27.180.8 (static). dig +short tti-solutions.com @8.8.8.8 → 34.27.180.8.
  • Cloud SQL (Postgres) via DATABASE_URL. Payroll field‑encryption key lives on a separate /app/secrets volume (never in DB backups), GUI‑rotated.
  • Compose for prod is always the two‑file overlay: -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml.
  • Infra/cutover notes: ttis-ai/docs/PRODUCTION_HOSTING.md, ttis-ai/docs/LMS_DEPLOY_AND_SEED_RUNBOOK.md.

⏰ Action by 2026‑08‑01 — Cloud SQL export IAM change. Google is removing the cloudsql.instances.export permission from the Viewer (roles/cloudsql.viewer) and legacy Reader roles. After that date, anyone (or any service account) holding only those roles can no longer export the database — via console, gcloud sql export, or the API. If a person or job exports the prod DB to Cloud Storage, grant them roles/cloudsql.editor (or a custom role that includes cloudsql.instances.export) before the date. Automated Cloud SQL backups are unaffected — this only touches the export‑to‑GCS path, so if you rely solely on managed backups, no action is needed. ```bash

Who currently has only Viewer / legacy Reader?

gcloud projects get-iam-policy tti-solutions-ai --flatten="bindings[].members" \ --filter="bindings.role=roles/cloudsql.viewer OR bindings.role=roles/cloudsql.legacyReader" \ --format="table(bindings.role, bindings.members)"

Grant export capability to whoever needs it:

gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding tti-solutions-ai \ --member="user:charles@tti-solutions.com" --role="roles/cloudsql.editor" ```

4.2 Connect to the prod VM

gcloud config set project tti-solutions-ai
gcloud compute ssh ttis-prod --zone=us-central1-a
cd ~/ttis-ai

4.3 Deploy — orchestrated (the safe default)

cd ~/ttis-ai
./scripts/deploy_prod.sh

Stages (aborts safely before any cutover if a check fails): preflight (docker + required .env keys) → repo sync (git pull --ff-only — ⚠️ watch the it pull typo, it silently no‑ops and leaves the backend stale) → Caddyfile validation (throwaway caddy validate with the domain env, incl. WEBHOOK_DOMAIN; a missing one fails validation — fixed in #196) → build+start app services (website stays live) → pull frontend from GHCR (bare up -d reuses cached :latest) → health‑gate /health → seed LMS catalog + policy‑ack (idempotent) → cutover Caddy (first run provisions Let's Encrypt; port 80 reachable).

4.4 Deploy — manual per‑service (the everyday path)

When you just need to ship the latest main for one service:

# Backend (dashboard) — pull + rebuild the image, recreate the container
git pull --ff-only origin main
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d --build dashboard

# Verify the new backend is serving (any 2xx/3xx is "up"; this endpoint is a quick probe)
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" localhost:8000/api/v1/projects/timeline

# Frontend — pull the CI-built GHCR image, recreate the container
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml pull frontend
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d frontend

# Force-recreate a service if a config/env change isn't picked up
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d --force-recreate dashboard

⚠️ After every RAG ingest you must rebuild the dashboard (the ingest writes to ChromaDB, but the running container won't reflect it until rebuilt) — see §7.5.

Rollback: docker compose stop caddy && docker start ttis-web.

4.5 Post‑deploy verification & precheck

# DNS resolves to the prod IP
dig +short tti-solutions.com @8.8.8.8                 # → 34.27.180.8

# Edge / TLS (run from anywhere)
curl -sI https://tti-solutions.com/                   # 200, Server: Caddy
curl -sI https://www.tti-solutions.com/               # 301 → https://tti-solutions.com
curl -sI https://app.tti-solutions.com/               # 307 → /vbs   (dashboard root redirects)
curl -sI https://app.tti-solutions.com/api/v1/health  # API health

# On the VM — container health (ttis-dashboard should read "(healthy)")
docker ps --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}'

# On the VM — certificate provisioning / renewal
docker logs ttis-caddy 2>&1 | grep -iE 'certificate obtained|obtaining|error' | tail -20

# On the VM — are any users provisioned? 0 ⇒ seed/create one (see §4.6), then restart dashboard
grep -c '^DASHBOARD_USERS=' config/.env
# if 0:
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d dashboard

4.6 Create a login on prod (on the VM)

User identities live in auth.db (not .env after first boot). Create/upsert a CEO login in place — the password hash is sha256(DASHBOARD_PWD_SALT + plaintext):

docker exec -e NU='charles' -e NP='ChangeThisPassword' ttis-dashboard python -c "
import os, hashlib
from dashboard import users
from dashboard.abac import Role
users.init_users_db()
pw = hashlib.sha256((os.environ['DASHBOARD_PWD_SALT'] + os.environ['NP']).encode()).hexdigest()
users.upsert_user(user_id='U-001', username=os.environ['NU'], password_sha256=pw,
                  role=Role.CEO, domain='tti-solutions.com')
print('created user:', os.environ['NU'], '(role ceo)')
"
# → created user: charles (role ceo)

Pass the password via the -e NP= env (don't bake it into the command history where avoidable), and change it on first login.

4.7 Caddy & domain routing (config/Caddyfile)

Domain Routes to Notes
tti-solutions.com static file_server (the built website/www) marketing apex
www.tti-solutions.com 301 → apex canonical redirect
app.tti-solutions.com /api/*,/health,/workflows/*,… → dashboard:8000; else → frontend:3000 flush_interval -1 keeps SSE/WS (Ask‑LYNNE) unbuffered
webhook.tti-solutions.com webhook:8001 Pursuite + Telegram inbound; DNS must point here

Validate after any edit:

caddy validate --config config/Caddyfile --adapter caddyfile \
  -e APP_DOMAIN=app.tti-solutions.com -e WEBSITE_DOMAIN=tti-solutions.com \
  -e ACME_EMAIL=admin@tti-solutions.com -e FRONTEND_UPSTREAM=frontend:3000 \
  -e WEBHOOK_DOMAIN=webhook.tti-solutions.com

4.8 Staging

python scripts/setup_staging_env.py        # writes config/.env.staging + 5 test logins (--force to regen)
# set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in config/.env.staging, then:
docker compose -p ttis-staging -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.staging.yml up -d

Staging runs on shifted host ports (dashboard 8100, webhook 8101, redis 6380, chromadb 8102) with isolated volumes — it never touches dev :8000. Logins e.g. staging_admin / Staging!CEO#2026.

4.9 Troubleshoot (prod)

Symptom Diagnose Fix
Deploy aborts at Caddy validation re‑read the validate error; usually a domain var Set WEBHOOK_DOMAIN/APP_DOMAIN/… in config/.env; re‑run.
New UI not visible after deploy docker inspect ttis-frontend --format '{{.Image}}' vs GHCR digest; browser cache docker compose -f … -f docker-compose.prod.yml pull frontend && … up -d frontend; hard‑refresh (Ctrl/Cmd‑Shift‑R).
New backend endpoints 404 git -C ~/ttis-ai log -1 --oneline (is main current?) git pull --ff-only origin main (beware it pull), then … up -d --build dashboard.
RAG results stale after ingest ingest ran but dashboard not rebuilt Rebuild: … up -d --build dashboard (see §7.5).
app.tti-solutions.com not 307→/vbs dashboard down / unhealthy docker ps → check ttis-dashboard (healthy); docker logs ttis-dashboard --tail 80.
Login fails for everyone / 0 users grep -c '^DASHBOARD_USERS=' config/.env = 0 and auth.db empty Create a user (§4.6); restart dashboard.
TLS cert errors on first cutover docker logs ttis-caddy 2>&1 \| grep -iE 'obtain\|error' Open ports 80/443 to the VM; wait for ACME challenge; re‑try.
Site serves old Squarespace / wrong IP dig +short tti-solutions.com @8.8.8.8 ≠ 34.27.180.8 Repoint DNS A records to 34.27.180.8; lower TTL ahead of changes.
Container unhealthy after deploy docker logs <name> --tail 120; check .env keys, Redis/Cloud SQL reachability Fix the missing env/dependency; … up -d --force-recreate <svc>.
Worker not draining jobs docker logs ttis-worker --tail 80; redis-cli llen queue:workflow_jobs Restart worker; ensure WORKFLOW_EXECUTION_MODE=queue + REDIS_URL correct.
Need to roll back the edge — docker compose stop caddy && docker start ttis-web.

4.10 Publish a confidential corpus doc to prod (e.g. the founder/company fact sheet)

corp_confidential corpus docs (the founder/company fact sheet, sensitive org docs) are gitignored by design — they never go to GitHub or the name‑scrubbed public corpus. So they ride to prod separately from the code, then get ingested + the dashboard rebuilt so LYNNE / the RESEARCHER agent ground on them. (The ingest handles .md, .docx, and .pdf in corp_confidential.)

Environment facts that bite (verified 2026‑06‑17): - GCP project is tti-solutions-ai (not ttis-ai — that's a different, empty project). VM ttis-prod, zone us-central1-a. - You SSH in as echar, but the app is deployed under user charles at /home/charles/ttis-ai (your ~ is /home/echar, which has no repo). So git/docker run as charles, and file ops into the repo need sudo. - The confidential dir is data/documents/corp_confidential/ — not …/library/corp_confidential/. The ingest globs …/corp_confidential/**/*.md and the container mounts ./data/documents:/app/data/documents:ro, so a file under a library/ subdir is silently skipped. - The ingest script is baked into the dashboard image (only data/documents is mounted, not scripts/). So you must build dashboard before ingesting or an older image runs the old script (e.g. one with no .md support). - On Windows, gcloud compute scp shells out to PuTTY's pscp, which reads a H:\… source as a remote host (the drive‑letter colon). cd into the file's folder and pass the bare filename. Close the file in any editor first (an Office ~$… lock → "Cannot open file").

# 0) (on the VM, as echar) make the real target dir, owned by charles
gcloud compute ssh ttis-prod --project=tti-solutions-ai --zone=us-central1-a
sudo install -d -o charles -g charles /home/charles/ttis-ai/data/documents/corp_confidential

# 1) (LOCAL, new PowerShell window — the SSH session holds the other) copy the gitignored doc to /tmp.
#    cd into the folder so pscp doesn't treat the H: drive letter as a remote host.
#    >>> run on your workstation, not the VM <<<
#    cd H:\Projects\TTI-S_VBS\ttis-ai\data\documents\corp_confidential
#    gcloud compute scp FOUNDER_COMPANY_FACT_SHEET.md ttis-prod:/tmp/ \
#        --project=tti-solutions-ai --zone=us-central1-a

# 2) (on the VM, as echar) move it into the repo with charles ownership
sudo install -o charles -g charles -m 644 \
  /tmp/FOUNDER_COMPANY_FACT_SHEET.md \
  /home/charles/ttis-ai/data/documents/corp_confidential/

# 3) (on the VM) switch to the charles user — owns the repo + can run docker/git
sudo -u charles -H bash -l
cd ~/ttis-ai
git pull --ff-only origin main                                  # get the .md‑capable ingest script
C="docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml"
$C build dashboard                                              # re‑bake the new ingest script INTO the image
$C run --rm --no-deps dashboard python scripts/ingest_rag_corpus.py   # ingest → live chromadb
#   look for:  corp_confidential   FOUNDER_COMPANY_FACT_SHEET.md → N chunks (md)
$C up -d dashboard                                              # restart on the rebuilt image

Verify: ask LYNNE a question only the sheet answers (e.g. "what year was TTIS founded?" → 2026, or "TTIS's initial commercial area of emphasis?" → data center infrastructure). LYNNE cites it as a REF‑n source. corp_confidential is CEO‑only and not name‑scrubbed, so personal facts are preserved. See the Corpus & RAG tab for the full ingest reference.

Notes: if git pull reports a dirty tree (an older root‑owned ingest_rag_corpus.py), git stash first. If docker says permission denied … docker.sock even as charles, prefix the $C commands with sudo. Chroma is a separate persisted service and the dashboard queries it live, so the ingest alone makes the doc searchable — the up -d dashboard just rolls onto the rebuilt image.

5. Marketing Site (www.tti-solutions.com)

Pure static site built from Markdown — no server, no framework. Source in ttis-ai/website/.

5.1 Build (produce the committed www/)

cd H:\Projects\TTI-S_VBS\ttis-ai\website
pip install -r requirements.txt
python build.py              # content/*.md (+ templates/base.html) → www/*.html, copies static/

5.2 Preview locally

Run the dev server from the repo root (not from website/):

cd H:\Projects\TTI-S_VBS\ttis-ai
py website\serve.py 8745

Open http://localhost:8745/. It rebuilds on each page navigation, so after editing content/*.md, templates/, or static/ just refresh the browser. Ctrl+C stops it.

Restart cleanly (free the port first, then start):

Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 8745 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
  Select-Object -ExpandProperty OwningProcess -Unique |
  ForEach-Object { Stop-Process -Id $_ -Force }
py website\serve.py 8745

5.3 Edit content

  • Pages are website/content/*.md with YAML front matter (title, slug, nav_order, description, hero_*, …). Layout helpers: ::: grid cols-3 …, ::: card, ::: cta-panel.
  • Add a page = add a content/<slug>.md with nav_order, run build.py, commit the rebuilt www/.
  • Before publishing, fill placeholders — see website/PLACEHOLDERS.md (CAGE, UEI, phone, email, capability‑statement PDF, Formspree endpoint). Check none remain: grep -rn "\[\[" website/content website/templates.

5.4 Production

Caddy serves the pre‑built www/ via file_server on the apex; www. 301‑redirects to it. The site migrated off Squarespace — deploy is just rebuilding www/ and shipping it with the stack (§4).

5.5 Troubleshoot

Symptom Diagnose Fix
serve.py errors / no pages run from website/ instead of repo root Run py website\serve.py 8745 from the repo root.
Port 8745 already in use another serve.py still running Free the port (the restart recipe in §5.2), then start again.
Edits not showing browser cached the page It rebuilds per navigation — refresh; hard‑refresh (Ctrl+F5) if needed.
Build fails: No module named markdown deps not installed pip install -r website/requirements.txt.
Placeholders visible on the live site [[…]] tokens not filled grep -rn "\[\[" website/content website/templates; fill per website/PLACEHOLDERS.md.

6. LMS

The LMS is a VBS module (dashboard/lms.py, dashboard/lms_db.py) surfaced through the Dashboard's LMS tabs (Courses, Review, Enrollments, Credentials, Escalations). Architecture/schema: docs/LMS_QMS_ARCHITECTURE.md; scope/decisions: docs/LMS_SCOPE.md.

6.1 Seed the catalog (idempotent)

docker exec ttis-dashboard python scripts/seed_lms_catalog.py            # seed if empty
docker exec ttis-dashboard python scripts/seed_lms_catalog.py --force    # re-apply (conflict-tolerant)
docker exec ttis-dashboard python scripts/seed_lms_catalog.py --print    # emit dialect SQL (pipe to psql)

Seeds courses, rubrics, certifications, curricula, the role→cert matrix, and registered competencies. Dialect‑portable (SQLite INSERT OR IGNORE / Postgres ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING). deploy_prod.sh runs this automatically.

6.2 Policy‑acknowledgment course (R‑ACK‑01/02)

docker exec ttis-dashboard python scripts/seed_policy_ack.py

Creates the POL-ACK course: a module + attestation quiz per company policy letter, plus a comprehension read‑gate for policies that have a question bank (scripts/policy_lessons/comprehension_banks.json): 5 random questions, 80% to pass; on fail the learner re‑reads and gets a different 5; 3 failures lock the quiz and escalate to a supervisor. Earns CERT-POLACK (365‑day). Idempotent (skips if POL-ACK exists).

Supervisor escalation / reset: - List open escalations: GET /api/v1/lms/quiz-escalations?status=open (also the Escalations tab). - Reset a locked learner: POST /api/v1/lms/enrollments/{enrollment_id}/quizzes/{quiz_id}/reset-lock.

6.3 Verify the LMS is live

  • After deploy/seed: the Dashboard Courses tab lists courses; the Review tab is present; a learner can open POL-ACK. If Courses is empty → run the catalog seed (§6.1). If the UI looks old → confirm the frontend image was pulled and hard‑refresh.

6.4 Corpus for the LMS

LMS authoring content is grounded in the same RAG corpus (the policies + corp_confidential collections) — see §7. The LMS authoring agent (P4b) drafts lessons grounded in the approved corpus and stages them through a three‑stage gate (competent reviewer → CEO release); it is unblocked once the policy .docx set is signed and ingested. Authoring guidance: docs/templates/CORPUS_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md; coverage: docs/LMS_CORPUS_COVERAGE_CHECKLIST.md.

7. Corpus & RAG Ingestion (VBS + LMS)

The RAG corpus grounds agent answers, LYNNE, and LMS authoring. Storage is ChromaDB (port 8002, data/chroma_db/) with hybrid retrieval (vector embeddings all-MiniLM-L6-v2 + BM25, fused by reciprocal‑rank). Query layer: dashboard/rag.py (access is enforced server‑side by ABAC capability).

7.1 Collections

Collection Source Visibility
far_dfars FAR/DFARS DITA‑HTML (H:/Projects/Library/FARHTML, DFARSHTML) all (name‑scrubbed)
sdvosb_regs 13 CFR Part 128 PDF (H:/Projects/Library/…) all (name‑scrubbed)
policies data/documents/policies/**/*.docx (+ allow‑listed .xlsx) all (name‑scrubbed)
corp_confidential data/documents/corp_confidential/** CEO‑only (not scrubbed)

7.2 Where corpus files go

ttis-ai/data/documents/
  policies/                 → policies collection (TTIS policy .docx, curriculum subfolders)
    Compliance/ Quality/ Safety/ Technical/ Travel/ curriculum/…
  corp_confidential/        → corp_confidential collection (CEO-only, not scrubbed)

Authoring a TTIS interpretive .docx: structure per docs/templates/CORPUS_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md (Policy Number header, numbered Heading‑1 sections). Do not paste verbatim OSHA/TIA/NEC/BICSI text (copyright wall) — those are external/attested credentials.

7.3 Ingest (idempotent)

The canonical run (throwaway container writing to the shared ttis-chromadb):

docker compose run --rm --no-deps \
  -e CHROMA_HOST=ttis-chromadb -e CHROMA_PORT=8000 \
  -v "H:/Projects/Library:/library:ro" \
  -v "H:/Projects/TTI-S_VBS/ttis-ai/data/documents:/app/data/documents:ro" \
  -v "H:/Projects/TTI-S_VBS/ttis-ai/tools:/app/tools:ro" \
  -v "H:/Projects/TTI-S_VBS/ttis-ai/dashboard:/app/dashboard:ro" \
  -v "H:/Projects/TTI-S_VBS/ttis-ai/scripts:/app/scripts:ro" \
  dashboard python scripts/ingest_rag_corpus.py

(Local non‑Docker equivalent: python main.py --ingest-docs.) Output logs per collection, e.g. policies 120 sections → 980 chunks ingested and a final total across collections.

Idempotent / re‑ingest: chunk IDs are deterministic (source_ref#chunk), so re‑running upserts in place — changed text gets new embeddings, unchanged chunks are silent overwrites. To update: edit the source doc, re‑run the same command. (Deleted docs leave orphan chunks until cleaned via the ChromaDB API.) FAR and DFARS share far_dfars, so always ingest in one pass.

Custom paths via env: RAG_LIBRARY_DIR (default /library), RAG_DOCS_DIR (default /app/data/documents), CHROMA_HOST/CHROMA_PORT.

7.4 Ingest on prod (background) + the mandatory rebuild

On the VM the corpus library is inside the container at /app/data/documents/library. Run the ingest detached (it's long‑running) and watch the log:

# Start the full ingest in the background
nohup docker exec -e RAG_LIBRARY_DIR=/app/data/documents/library ttis-dashboard \
  python scripts/ingest_rag_corpus.py > ~/ingest.log 2>&1 &

tail -f ~/ingest.log                     # watch progress (Ctrl-C to stop watching)
tail -n 15 ~/ingest.log                  # last lines
pgrep -af ingest_rag_corpus | grep -v grep || echo "ingest finished"

Ingest a single collection (e.g. re‑load the SDVOSB CFR PDF) without a full run:

docker exec ttis-dashboard python -c "from tools.rag_ingest import load_cfr_pdf; from dashboard.rag import chunk_sections, ingest_chunks; s=list(load_cfr_pdf('/app/data/documents/library/13 CFR Part 125.pdf','13 CFR Part 125')); print('sdvosb_regs:', ingest_chunks('sdvosb_regs', chunk_sections(s,'sdvosb_regs')), 'chunks')"

7.5 Rebuild the dashboard after EVERY ingest

Ingest writes to ChromaDB, but the running dashboard caches its retrievers and won't serve the new corpus until rebuilt:

cd ~/ttis-ai && git pull --ff-only origin main
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d --build dashboard

7.6 Verify

After ingest and the rebuild, run a RAG search from the Dashboard Knowledge tab (or the LYNNE/agent path) and confirm citations resolve to the new sections.

8. Ask‑LYNNE

LYNNE is the CEO‑facing conversational EAA (Claude Opus), with grounded RAG citations, tool use, and propose→confirm workflow initiation. Code: dashboard/lynne_stream.py (orchestrator), dashboard/lynne_tools.py, dashboard/lynne_actions.py, dashboard/lynne_projects.py.

8.1 Access

  • Dashboard: the Ask‑LYNNE tab (streaming chat + citations).
  • SSE endpoint: POST /api/v1/lynne/stream (events: start, citations, token…, done).
  • Telegram: the LYNNE bot (same backend via the webhook receiver).

8.2 Configuration (env)

Variable Default Purpose
LYNNE_ALLOWED_PRINCIPALS unset Allow‑list of principals (opt‑in restriction).
LYNNE_WEB_SEARCH 1 Live web search tool.
LYNNE_PROJECTS 1 GRID executive‑project tools.
LYNNE_MEMORY 1 Rich cross‑turn memory (recall earlier research/docs).
LYNNE_OUTPUT_FORMAT / LYNNE_OUTPUT_EMAIL gdoc / unset Save‑to‑Drive format / email recipient.
GOOGLE_DRIVE_LYNNE_FOLDER / GOOGLE_DRIVE_SHARED_ID unset Drive target for saved runs.
Durable runs — LYNNE_STEP_GATES, LYNNE_STEP_GATE_TIMEOUT, LYNNE_RUN_TTL_SECONDS, LYNNE_SEQ_COMPACTION, LYNNE_SEQ_COMPACT_TOKENS, LYNNE_DIGEST_MODEL.
Tool keys unset GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY (maps/places), TICKETMASTER_API_KEY (events), DUFFEL_API_TOKEN (travel). Tool appears only if its key is set; weather (Weather.gov) needs none.

8.3 Google Workspace (Gmail/Calendar/Drive) for LYNNE

python scripts/setup_google_workspace.py     # interactive: GCP project, enable APIs, service account,
                                             # domain-wide delegation, env vars, Docker mount

It configures a service account + domain‑wide delegation and prints the env vars to add (GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON, GOOGLE_IMPERSONATED_USER, Drive folder IDs). Mount ./secrets read‑only into the container. Verify:

docker exec ttis-dashboard python -c "from tools.google_gmail import GoogleGmail; print(len(GoogleGmail().list_emails(max_results=3)))"

8.4 Capabilities

  • Read tools (inline): weather, directions/places (Maps), travel search (Duffel), Drive/Gmail/Calendar read, web search.
  • Propose→confirm: initiate workflow, sync deadlines to calendar, delegate research.
  • Direct (audit‑logged): GRID project create/update/delete, calendar event cleanup.
  • Playbooks: savable templated multi‑step tasks (Ask‑LYNNE + Telegram).
  • Durable runs: Redis‑buffered, survive disconnect, optional save‑to‑Drive; mid‑run confirm gates.
  • Integrity guardrails (agents/integrity_policy.py): anti‑assumption (no unsourced identity/authority claims), confidence scoring, needs_review flag below threshold.

8.5 Troubleshoot

Symptom Cause Fix
"Save to Drive" fails Workspace not configured Re‑run setup_google_workspace.py; check GOOGLE_* env + Drive folder ID.
A tool isn't offered its API key is unset Set GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY / DUFFEL_API_TOKEN / etc.; restart.
Web search missing LYNNE_WEB_SEARCH=0 Set =1, restart.
Streaming arrives in bursts Caddy buffering Ensure flush_interval -1 on the dashboard proxy in config/Caddyfile.
429 rate‑limit Anthropic tier/limit LYNNE auto‑retries with back‑off; raise the org token‑per‑minute limit if persistent.

9. Pursuite (GovCon capture/proposal platform)

Separate product at H:\Projects\Pursuite\app — an npm‑workspaces monorepo (apps/api, apps/web; internally codenamed BidCommandPro). It feeds the TTIS VBS through the Pursuite webhook (CRM events → port 8001). Local dev runs in Docker; config is injected via env_file: .env (see the Docker Desktop / WSL2 .env gotcha in §1.4). Prod images are tagged pursuite/api:YYYYMMDD. The admin/troubleshooting CLI lives in apps/api/src/db/scripts/ and all helper scripts run from apps/api/ (they read the monorepo‑root .env automatically). When in doubt, run npm run check-health first (§9.7).

Two sibling dirs, easy to confuse: src/db/seeds/ = seeding scripts (run once to populate a demo/dev env); src/db/scripts/ = admin CLI tools (run on‑demand to inspect/modify existing data).

9.1 Local run & container control (Docker)

cd H:\Projects\Pursuite\app
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.localtest.yml up -d --force-recreate
docker compose ps

# Per-container control
docker compose up -d --force-recreate web    # recreate the web container
docker compose restart api                    # restart the API
docker compose logs api -f                     # follow API logs
docker compose logs api --tail=5               # confirm API status

Always recreate rather than restart (… up -d --force-recreate, or down --remove-orphans then up). Restarting a stale container replays the old single‑file .env mount and fails — see §1.4.

Log in via the web UI (local-auth): there is no separate username field. Set Organization to default, click "Admin only? Use local login", then enter the admin email and local-admin password. On a clean standalone install Pursuite Manager auto-creates admin@pursuite.local in org default and writes the first-run password to %APPDATA%\Pursuite\first-run-admin.txt. Use that first-run password once, then create a named admin account and rotate/clear the temporary local password (see section 9.9).

Running CLI scripts against local infra needs the DB + Redis env present in the shell:

$env:DATABASE_URL = 'postgresql://bidcommand:devpassword@localhost:5432/bidcommand'   # local dev only
$env:REDIS_URL    = 'redis://localhost:6379'

9.2 Seed TTI‑Solutions' own tenant (telecom bootstrap + market intel)

The seed scripts live in the apps/api workspace, so run them from there (or add -w apps/api from the monorepo root):

cd H:\Projects\Pursuite\app\apps\api

# 1) Bootstrap company data — NAICS 238210 (wiring), 517919 (telecom), 561621 (security systems)
npm run seed:tti -- --org tti-solutions --email charles@tti-solutions.com --password "YourPW" `
  --naics 238210,517919,561621 --opportunities 6

# 2) Pull real market intelligence from USASpending.gov for that org
npm run seed:enrich -- --org tti-solutions

# 3) Refresh market data periodically
npm run seed:enrich -- --org tti-solutions --reset

Direct equivalent: npx tsx src/db/seeds/telecom-bootstrap.ts --org tti-solutions ….

9.3 Demo seeds (sales / AI‑positioning demos)

cd H:\Projects\Pursuite\app\apps\api

# Simplest — named org with a local-auth login
npm run seed:ai-demo -- --org demo-ai --email admin@example.com --password "Demo@2026!" --naics 541512

# Reset an existing named org (purge + re-seed)
npm run seed:ai-demo -- --org demo-ai --reset --email admin@example.com --password "Demo@2026!"

# No flags = auto-generated unique slug, default email, no local-auth password
npm run seed:ai-demo

Flags: --org <slug> (named; omit → auto unique slug), --email/--password (local‑auth login; omit → none), --reset (purge the named --org then re‑seed; omitting --org always creates fresh), --naics <csv>. Direct: npx tsx src/db/seeds/ai-positioning-demo.ts ….

What the AI demo builds — a 3‑bid / 2‑agency story arc that exercises the three AI initiatives: CISA CDM Phase 4 (LOSS to Booz Allen, with a written debrief) → Army DEVCOM MCADE (WIN $12.8M, banked win themes) → CISA ZTA Advisory (ACTIVE, due in 28 days, the test bed) → NIH IT Modernization (QUALIFYING). It demonstrates (1) Capture‑Context Injection (win themes, debriefs, CRM hot buttons, bid/no‑bid rationale, compliance scan, content library), (2) Agentic Content Research (real agency DHS CISA + real NAICS 541512 → live USASpending.gov awarded‑contract queries), and (3) Section‑Type Intelligent Pipelines (six section types, each with type‑specific context; Price/Cost links to the CPQ quote). Using a real agency + NAICS is what makes USASpending return actual awards in the demo.

Generic CRM test data (separate from the tenant/demo seeds — bulk accounts + contacts for load/UX testing). Two scripts in G:\BidCommandPro\seed\: run generate.js first (→ accounts.csv 100 rows + contacts.csv ~1,615 rows; 10 "anchor" accounts get a 5‑level org chart), then seed.js to POST them to a running API:

node generate.js
node seed.js --url http://localhost:3001 --token <jwt>   # --dry to preview, --delay 50 to throttle

--token is a JWT from POST /api/v1/auth/login. seed.js imports in dependency order (accounts → contacts → wire reportsTo hierarchy) with a live progress bar.

9.4 Reset a local‑auth password

npm run reset-local-pw -- --email david.chen@meridianfed.example `
  --org meridian-federal-solutions --password "Demo@Pursuite2026!"

9.5 Demo playbooks (sales enablement)

Playbook Location Contents
Sales Demo G:\Pursuite\marketing\Pursuite_Sales_Demo_Playbook.docx / .pdf 10 modules, ~64 min — talking points, discovery questions, objection handling, pricing‑tier table, demo setup checklist.
AI Demo G:\Pursuite\marketing\Pursuite_AI_Demo_Playbook.docx / .pdf 3 initiative deep‑dives (color‑coded banners), live proof‑point boxes, competitive positioning table, pre‑seeded checklist tied to the seed script.

9.6 Build & deliver (customer packaging)

cd H:\Projects\Pursuite\app
.\scripts\build-and-deliver.ps1

Interactive menu (before build): APP URL (required), Admin email (required), Org slug (default default), Temp password (auto‑generated, 16 random chars). Five stages:

  1. Write APP_URL + APP_VERSION to .env.production.
  2. docker compose build — prod images tagged pursuite/api:YYYYMMDD.
  3. Delivery gate — bootstrap-admin + the vitest regression suite. If this fails, no package is produced.
  4. Package assembly → delivery/pursuite-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.zip: docker-compose.production.yml, pre‑configured .env.production, migrations/ (26 SQL files), images/ (docker‑save tarballs of both images), MANIFEST.txt.
  5. Verdict banner — App URL, admin username, org slug, temp password (shown once — capture it), "pw expires 3 days after first login", package path, size (~842 MB), SHA‑256.

9.7 Health check (run this first)

cd H:\Projects\Pursuite\app\apps\api
npm run check-health

One command checks DB connectivity, the critical schema columns, Redis ping, and BullMQ queue depths. Exit 0 = all clear, 1 = one or more failures (safe for CI/monitoring). A red ❌ Schema: <table>.<column> line means a migration didn't run — go to §9.10.

9.8 Helper / admin scripts (apps/api/src/db/scripts/)

Run from apps/api/. Full option reference is in each script's JSDoc header.

Script Purpose
npm run check-health DB + schema + Redis + BullMQ health (exit 0/1).
npm run list-orgs Table of every org: slug, plan, Stripe status, trial expiry, user count.
npm run inspect-org -- --org <slug> Deep‑inspect one org — billing, full user table (roles/active/SSO), anomaly warnings.
npm run create-user -- --email <e> --org <slug> --role <role> [--name "…"] Create a user.
npm run set-user-role -- --email <e> --org <slug> --role <role> Change a user's role.
npm run reset-local-pw -- --email <e> --org <slug> --password <pw> Set/reset the bcrypt local‑auth password (admin only).
npm run clear-session -- --email <e> --org <slug> Force‑logout (writes Redis blocklist).
npm run set-plan -- --org <slug> --plan <plan> [opts] Override an org's plan/billing for testing.
npm run purge:org -- [--pattern <pre>\|--org <slug>\|--keep <csv>] [--dry-run] Targeted org purge.
npm run purge:test Purge test-* orgs + clear business data from the bootstrap org.
npm run db:generate / npm run db:migrate Generate / apply Drizzle migrations.

9.9 Users, roles & sessions

cd H:\Projects\Pursuite\app\apps\api
npm run create-user -- --email admin@pursuite.local --org pursuite --role admin
npm run create-user -- --email writer@example.com --org pursuite --role writer --name "Jane Doe"
npm run set-user-role -- --email charles@pursuite.io --org pursuite --role admin
npm run reset-local-pw -- --email admin@pursuite.local --org pursuite --password "<password>"

Valid roles: admin · proposal_manager · writer · executive_approver · read_only. Only admin can use local‑auth login. First‑time SSO users are auto‑provisioned read_only — upgrade via Admin → Users.

Bootstrap an admin from env (fresh org, dev/source via tsx):

$env:ADMIN_EMAIL='admin@pursuite.local'; $env:ADMIN_PASSWORD='<password>'
$env:ORG_SLUG='pursuite'; $env:ORG_NAME='Pursuite'
npx tsx apps/api/src/db/seeds/bootstrap-admin.ts

Standalone delivered app login and named-admin handoff. The standalone login form has no separate username field. Use:

  • Org: default
  • Email: admin email
  • Password: local admin password

On a clean standalone install, Pursuite Manager auto-creates:

Email: admin@pursuite.local
Org: default
Password file: %APPDATA%\Pursuite\first-run-admin.txt

Use that first-run password to sign in, then create your named admin account.

To create/set a named admin from PowerShell against the standalone container:

docker exec pursuite-api node dist/db/scripts/create-user.js --email "you@company.com" --org default --role admin --name "Your Name"

docker exec pursuite-api node dist/db/scripts/reset-local-pw.js --email "you@company.com" --org default --password "Use-A-Strong-Password-Here"

If the user already exists, promote them and set/reset the password:

docker exec pursuite-api node dist/db/scripts/set-user-role.js --email "you@company.com" --org default --role admin

docker exec pursuite-api node dist/db/scripts/reset-local-pw.js --email "you@company.com" --org default --password "Use-A-Strong-Password-Here"

After confirming the named admin can log in, disable the temporary/default local password:

docker exec pursuite-api node dist/db/scripts/reset-local-pw.js --email admin@pursuite.local --org default --clear

Gotcha: standalone-admin.js only creates the first admin if the org has no existing admin. Once admin@pursuite.local exists, use create-user, set-user-role, and reset-local-pw; rerunning standalone-admin.js will not reset the password.

reset-local-pw specifics: local‑auth is admin‑only (systemRole = "admin"); the password is a bcrypt hash (12 rounds) stored in Redis at local_pw:<userId>. --clear deletes it (disables local auth for that user); does not affect SSO.

Force‑logout / "Token revoked": clear-session writes blocklist entries for the access token (blocklist:<userId>, 8 h) and refresh token (blocklist:<userId>:refresh, 30 d); the next request returns 401 Token revoked. A normal login clears them automatically. It accepts --email <e> --org <slug> or --user-id <uuid>. Stale entries after re‑login: redis-cli DEL blocklist:<userId> blocklist:<userId>:refresh.

9.10 Schema drift & migrations (the #1 login‑500 cause)

Symptom: 500 on POST /api/v1/auth/local with column "X" does not exist (e.g. onboarding_completed_at, settings, system_roles, notification_prefs). A migration added columns but didn't run against this DB.

# Apply pending migrations
cd H:\Projects\Pursuite\app\apps\api
npm run db:migrate                       # or: npm run db:migrate --workspace=@bidcommand/api
npm run check-health                     # all Schema rows should be green

Emergency manual fix (CI down / first deploy) via psql:

ALTER TABLE organizations ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS onboarding_completed_at timestamp;
ALTER TABLE organizations ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS settings jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}';
ALTER TABLE organizations ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS plan text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'free';
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS system_roles text[];
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS sso_subject text;
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS notification_prefs jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}';

Prevent: run db:migrate in the deploy pipeline before the API process starts.

9.11 Onboarding wizard & org cleanup

# Onboarding wizard (clears/sets onboardingCompletedAt) — run from monorepo root
node --env-file=.env node_modules/.bin/tsx apps/api/src/db/scripts/reset-onboarding.ts            # force reappear
node --env-file=.env node_modules/.bin/tsx apps/api/src/db/scripts/reset-onboarding.ts --org acme  # specific org
node --env-file=.env node_modules/.bin/tsx apps/api/src/db/scripts/reset-onboarding.ts --complete  # suppress wizard

# Purge orgs (from apps/api) — ALWAYS --dry-run first
npm run purge:org -- --pattern test- --dry-run
npm run purge:org -- --pattern test-
npm run purge:org -- --pattern tti-solutions-17            # all timestamp-suffixed TTI seeds
npm run purge:org -- --keep default,meridian-federal-solutions --dry-run
npm run purge:org -- --org tti-solutions-1716300000000     # one exact slug
npm run purge:test                                          # test-* + clear bootstrap-org business data

9.12 Billing / plan overrides (testing)

cd H:\Projects\Pursuite\app\apps\api
npm run set-plan -- --plan enterprise
npm run set-plan -- --plan pro --status active --period-end +30d
npm run set-plan -- --plan starter --status trialing --trial-ends +3d   # trial banner
npm run set-plan -- --plan free --status canceled --trial-ends -1d      # red "upgrade required"
npm run set-plan -- --plan free --clear-stripe                          # reset / unsubscribe
npm run set-plan -- --org <slug> --plan pro                             # target a specific org

Plan not updating after Stripe checkout? Check: webhook delivered (checkout.session.completed)? STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET correct (a wrong secret fails signature verify silently)? STRIPE_STARTER_PRICE_ID + STRIPE_PRO_PRICE_ID both set and matching the dashboard? Inspect with npm run inspect-org -- --org <slug>.

9.13 Redis & background jobs

redis-cli ping                                   # → PONG (else Redis down / wrong REDIS_URL)
redis-cli KEYS "bull:*:wait"                      # waiting BullMQ jobs
redis-cli ZCARD "bull:sam-monitor:delayed"         # scheduled SAM.gov monitors
redis-cli LMOVE "bull:<q>:failed" "bull:<q>:wait" LEFT RIGHT   # retry a failed job

SAM.gov monitors run as BullMQ scheduled jobs; each org supplies its own SAM.gov API key (Admin → Integrations, expires annually). For managed Redis use rediss:// (TLS) and check VPC rules.

9.14 Environment variables (reference)

All belong in the monorepo‑root .env (or your secrets manager).

Variable Required Notes
APP_URL ✅ Public base URL users type to reach the app, no trailing slash. See the callout below.
DATABASE_URL ✅ postgres://user:pass@host/db.
REDIS_URL ✅ redis://… or rediss://… (TLS).
JWT_SECRET ✅ ≥32 random chars.
TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY ✅ Distinct 64‑char hex (openssl rand -hex 32), ≠ JWT_SECRET; server fails closed if missing/dup.
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY / STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET ✅ sk_… / whsec_….
STRIPE_STARTER_PRICE_ID / STRIPE_PRO_PRICE_ID ✅ Must match the Stripe dashboard exactly.
EMAIL_PROVIDER (+ provider keys) ✅ ses (SES_FROM_ADDRESS, AWS_*) or sendgrid (SENDGRID_*).
VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY / VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY / VAPID_SUBJECT push npx web-push generate-vapid-keys (changing them forces re‑subscribe).
AWS_S3_BUCKET / AWS_S3_REGION / AWS_* uploads S3 file storage.
OIDC_ISSUER / OIDC_CLIENT_ID / OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET / OIDC_REDIRECT_URI SSO SSO_PROVIDER=auth0 or generic.

APP_URL — the most delivery‑critical setting. It's the public base URL the API uses to build absolute links/redirects in five places: user‑invitation accept links, Stripe checkout success_url/cancel_url, the Stripe billing‑portal return_url, Nylas calendar/email OAuth callbacks, and "View Report" links in scheduled‑report emails (plus the desktop tray's open‑in‑browser). Set it to exactly what users type to reach the app — no trailing slash. If it's wrong, invite emails contain broken links, Stripe redirects fail after checkout, and OAuth redirects go to the wrong host. Examples: http://localhost (local), http://192.168.1.100 (LAN/office — most common self‑hosted), http://bidcommandpro.local (custom DNS/hosts), https://bids.yourcompany.com (Internet + TLS).

9.15 Error‑code quick reference

HTTP Message Likely cause See
401 Token revoked force‑logout / stale blocklist §9.9
401 Invalid credentials wrong pw, user inactive, or not admin §9.9
400 Invalid or expired state SSO state TTL (60 s) expired §9.13
400 Organization 'X' not found bad ?org= slug on /auth/login list-orgs
403 Plan limit exceeded free/starter hitting a gated feature §9.12
500 column "X" does not exist migration not applied §9.10
500 TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY error missing/duplicate key §9.14
500 connect ECONNREFUSED Redis/Postgres not reachable §9.13

9.16 Troubleshoot (operations + product)

Symptom Diagnose Fix
OCI not a directory on .env mount stale container replaying old single‑file mount down --remove-orphans then up -d --force-recreate (§1.4).
Login 500 read the API log stack trace (docker compose logs api --tail=50) Almost always schema drift (§9.10) or a missing env key (§9.14).
Login 401 despite correct password inspect-org — user inactive / not admin? Reactivate (UPDATE users SET active=true …); only admin does local‑auth (§9.9).
Seed says org already exists named --org already seeded Add --reset (or omit --org for a fresh slug).
USASpending returns nothing unreal agency/NAICS, or org not seeded Seed first; use real agency + NAICS (demo uses DHS CISA + 541512).
Delivery gate fails in build-and-deliver.ps1 bootstrap-admin/vitest failed Fix the failing test before packaging — the gate is intentional.
Can't log in after delivery temp password expired (3 days) / not captured Shown once in the verdict banner; re‑issue via reset-local-pw.
"MFA code required" but never enrolled account is TOTP‑enrolled Enter the authenticator code; Admin can reset MFA (Admin → Users), then re‑enroll.
MFA code rejected repeatedly device clock drift Wait for the next 30‑s code; set phone time to automatic.
Document blocked — "clearance required" (403) clearance below the item minimum Set under Profile → Security (Admin‑assigned); the denial is audited.
RFP Shredder finds no requirements scanned/image‑only PDF, OCR layer absent OCR runs automatically; re‑upload a clearer scan or add requirements manually (air‑gap bundles the OCR layer).
Compliance Matrix export stuck "Generating" DOCX/PDF renders in the background Wait for Complete (CSV is instant); ensure soffice (LibreOffice) is on the worker for PDF.
No emails sent / going to spam check EMAIL_PROVIDER + keys; SPF/DKIM/DMARC SES sandbox only sends to verified addresses — request production access.

9.17 Mutative Test Harness & regression

MTH (testing/mutative-harness/; ref MTH_User_Guide_v4) — adversarial input mutation against the Pursuite API. Never run against prod — it creates/deletes users and edits org config; use a dedicated test/staging instance.

cd H:\Projects\Pursuite\testing\mutative-harness
npm install ; copy .env.example .env     # add tokens + API URL (DRY_RUN=true by default)
npm run run:dry                          # plan + results/latest.html, no requests sent
# set DRY_RUN=false in .env, then a scope:
npm run run:all                          # or run:auth / run:rbac / run:compliance / run:admin

Mutation types: Structural (remove_field / empty_object / extra_fields / nesting), Type (invalid_enum SQLi / binary), Value (sql / xss / path‑traversal / long‑string), Semantic (cross_org_id), Protocol (missing_auth / wrong_role_token / alg=none / malformed), Chaos (duplicate / concurrent / large_payload), Fuzz. Findings → results/latest.{json,html}; severities CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW; exit 0 clean / 1 CRITICAL (block) / 2 fatal. Add an endpoint = append a baseline in src/input-generator/baseline-payloads.ts (the engine auto‑generates all groups).

17‑step regression pipeline — scripts/run-regression.ps1 (ref TestPlan_Regression_v6.docx; 301 documented TCs, 258 active):

.\scripts\run-regression.ps1                  # full 17-step run
.\scripts\run-regression.ps1 -Filter "BUG-"   # bug-fix regression only
.\scripts\run-regression.ps1 -Filter "NEG-" -ApiOnly
.\scripts\run-regression.ps1 -AirGap -AirGapApiUrl http://airgap-host:3000 -AirGapEmail admin@company.gov

Steps: prereqs → container health → credentials → tsc → API vitest → web vitest → BUG‑/NEG‑ regression → CPQ → debrief/WLA → RFP shredder → A‑series stubs → phase 1–4 suites → air‑gap smoke (25 checks) → summary (test-results/regression-*.txt). Flags: -ApiOnly / -WebOnly / -Filter / -AirGap. Filter aliases: BUG-, NEG-, TC-CPQ-, TC-DB-, TC-RFP-, TC-WLA-, …

The TTIS VBS port of this methodology lives on the TTI‑VBS tab §1.11 (tools/mth/, scripts/run_regression.py) — same oracle/strategy model, adapted to FastAPI + the ABAC PDP.

Appendix

A · Kill a stuck listening port (Windows / PowerShell)

Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 8081 -State Listen |
  Select-Object -ExpandProperty OwningProcess -Unique |
  ForEach-Object { Stop-Process -Id $_ -Force }

(Swap 8081 for 8000/3000/etc. as needed.)

B · Quick command index

Task Command
Connect to prod gcloud config set project tti-solutions-ai → gcloud compute ssh ttis-prod --zone=us-central1-a
Prod deploy backend git pull --ff-only origin main → docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d --build dashboard
Prod deploy frontend docker compose -f … -f docker-compose.prod.yml pull frontend → … up -d frontend
Prod verify edge curl -sI https://app.tti-solutions.com/ (307→/vbs) · dig +short tti-solutions.com @8.8.8.8 (34.27.180.8)
Prod health docker ps --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}'
Create prod user docker exec -e NU=… -e NP=… ttis-dashboard python -c "…upsert_user…" (§4.6)
VBS dev up python main.py + uvicorn dashboard.main:app --port 8000 --env-file config\.env + uvicorn tools.pursuite_webhook:webhook_app --port 8001
Reset CEO password (local) …python.exe -c "…set_password/upsert_user…" (§1.8)
VBS docker up docker-compose up -d
VBS tests pytest tests/ -q
Verify audit chain python main.py --verify-chain
Dashboard dev npm run dev (:3000)
Dashboard checks npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test:cov && npm run build
Field app dev npm run start (Expo)
Field app build eas build --platform ios|android
Deploy prod ./scripts/deploy_prod.sh (on the VM)
Validate Caddy caddy validate --config config/Caddyfile --adapter caddyfile -e …
Staging up docker compose -p ttis-staging -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.staging.yml up -d
Build website python website/build.py → preview python website/serve.py
Seed LMS docker exec ttis-dashboard python scripts/seed_lms_catalog.py
Seed policy‑ack docker exec ttis-dashboard python scripts/seed_policy_ack.py
Ingest corpus docker compose run --rm … dashboard python scripts/ingest_rag_corpus.py
LYNNE Workspace setup python scripts/setup_google_workspace.py
